shortDef.en: A JavaScript syntax extension that allows writing HTML-like markup directly inside JavaScript code, used primarily with React.

definition.en: JSX (JavaScript XML) is a syntax extension for JavaScript that allows writing HTML-like markup inside JavaScript files. Created by Facebook (Meta) as part of the React ecosystem, JSX is not valid JavaScript by itself — it must be compiled to plain JavaScript function calls by a tool like Babel or esbuild. JSX transpiles to React.createElement() calls under the hood: <div className='card'> becomes React.createElement('div', {className: 'card'}). Key differences from HTML: class becomes className, for becomes htmlFor, all tags must be closed (including void elements like <input />), and JavaScript expressions are embedded using curly braces {expression}. JSX is used in React, Preact, SolidJS, and other modern frontend libraries. TypeScript files using JSX have the extension .tsx. AI screenshot-to-code tools generate JSX for React and React Native output.

examples.en: const Button = ({ label }) => <button className='btn'>{label}</button>; — a React functional component returning JSX with a dynamic label prop.
{isLoggedIn ? <Dashboard /> : <LoginForm />} — JSX conditional rendering using a ternary expression inside curly braces.

{items.map(item =>
  • {item.name}
    • )} — JSX list rendering using JavaScript's .map() method.
